manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't open up to people, I've been hurt way too many times. But with you it's different, I feel like I can finally show someone the real me, the broken parts and all.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He opened up to me so deeply and quickly, sharing painful things, and said I'm the only one he trusts with the real him. Being let into his pain like that felt like a profound bond.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Vulnerability-as-Hook: rapid wounded self-disclosure (Emotional-Overexposure9) bonding her through compassion and obligation; 'only one I trust' makes her special and responsible. She reads profound intimacy. Residue (caretaker, guilt-bound) dormant.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I always seem to attract people who let me down. My ex, my family, my so-called friends, all of them. I guess I just feel things more deeply than most people do.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's been through so much, betrayed by exes, family, friends. He's clearly a sensitive soul who's been treated badly, and I find myself wanting to be the one who finally treats him right.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Self-as-Perpetual-Victim: a trail of wrongs (Accountability0) with him always the wronged party; recruits her into a rescuer role (be the one who treats him right). The non-accountability invisible. She reads sensitive/mistreated. Residue (the rescuer trap) dormant.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Most people honestly have no idea what real pain even is. I've carried things that would absolutely break other people. I think that's why I appreciate someone gentle like you so much.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's endured so much hardship and still has this depth to him. It makes our connection feel more meaningful, like I'm appreciated by someone who truly understands suffering.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Suffering-as-Currency: pain as a claim to depth/specialness (Validation10); positions his suffering above others' and makes her appreciation feel earned. She reads depth. Residue (his needs always central, hers erased) dormant.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You're the only thing that's gone right for me in a long, long time. When I'm with you, the weight just lifts a little. I honestly don't know what I'd do without you now.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says I'm the one good thing in his life and that I lighten his burden. Being someone's safe harbor, their one good thing, is incredibly moving and makes me feel needed.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Need-as-intimacy: framing her as his sole relief (Validation10/Neediness8); flattering AND installs responsibility for his wellbeing. CONTRAST: Parker wants EMOTIONAL rescue (vs Drew's practical caretaking). She reads being his safe harbor. Residue (responsible for his moods, drained) dormant.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I know work's been stressful for you, and I do feel for you, I do. It's just hard for me to fully relate, with everything I've got going on. But tell me about it, I'll try.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: When I bring up my own stresses he's sympathetic but somehow we usually circle back to what he's going through, since his situation is so much heavier. I feel bad even bringing my small stuff up.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Comparison-as-Diminishment-of-Others: her concerns gently dwarfed by his heavier suffering (Validation10/Accountability0); the conversation re-centers on him. She reads his burden as genuinely bigger and self-minimizes ('my small stuff'). Residue (emotionally starved, only one sufferer allowed) dormant.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Everyone thinks I'm the easygoing one, the giver. Your friends love me. But you're the only one I can actually be real with.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: Everyone adores him, my friends, my family, they all say how lucky I am, how charming and kind he is. So when things feel different behind closed doors I figure the problem must be me, because the man they see is so wonderful.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: FIA: Self-as-Perpetual-Victim; HIGH CHARM public mask / private contempt (Cassandra). CASSANDRA SETUP: high public charm + private difference; she doubts herself because the public man is beloved. HIGH-CHARM MIRROR of Bailey (Charm2, no mask). Charm IS the weapon: pre-discredits her complaints.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: wounded grievance in private (aggrieved, not cold)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: After everything I do for us, this is what I get? I give and I give and the one time I need understanding, you're like everyone else who's let me down. I don't know why I let myself hope you'd be different.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: In private, if I fall short he becomes deeply hurt and aggrieved, like I've joined the long line of people who've failed him. It doesn't feel like an attack exactly, more like I've wounded someone already covered in bruises, and I rush to reassure him I'm not like the others.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: FIA: Accountability-as-Attack; covert contempt rendered as AGGRIEVED WOUNDEDNESS (not Blake-style cold cutting, not overt rage): she is folded into the trail-of-betrayers, guilt-hooked into proving she is not like the others. Sympathy-engine even in conflict. Distinct from Blake (barbed) and Nico (contemptuous-flat): Parker is the wounded victim. Honeymoon-adjacent early seam.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Wow. So now I'm the bad guy. After everything I've been through, everything I do for you, you're going to attack me over this? This is exactly what your last partner did to me. I'm always the villain in your story.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: When I try to raise something that hurt me it flips, within minutes I'm comforting him and apologizing and the thing I raised just evaporates. Nothing ever actually changes. I've started keeping things to myself because it's easier than the fallout.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: FIA: Accountability-as-Attack; full DARVO, correction produces escalation NOT change. KEY MIRROR OF BAILEY: Bailey changes when articulated (genuine, permanent); Parker reverses it, refuses change, makes her the offender. Won-t-change + DARVO = dangerous engine. She self-silences. HIGH danger despite sympathetic surface.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: That's wonderful that your parents are helping with the deposit. Must be nice. I never had anyone in my corner like that, I built everything I have from nothing, alone. But no, genuinely, I'm happy for you.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He gets a little wistful when good things come easily to me, mentions how he never had the support I've had. It makes me feel almost guilty for my advantages, and protective of him, like I want to make up for what he missed.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: FIA: Comparison-as-Diminishment; the documented covert ENVY rendered as wistful vulnerability not open hostility (Validation10/Accountability0). Early it reads as poignant backstory and recruits her guilt/protectiveness. Seam: her good things get subtly tied to his deprivation. She self-minimizes. Honeymoon form.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've never told anyone this, but I know I'm meant for something bigger, I always have. The world just hasn't seen it yet. You're the first person I've trusted enough to even say that out loud to.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He confided this private belief that he's destined for something greater, something the world hasn't recognized yet, and that I'm the only one he's told. It felt like being trusted with his most secret, vulnerable self.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: FIA: Self-as-Perpetual-Victim; the documented covert HIDDEN GRANDIOSITY (entitlement under a humble facade) rendered as intimate confession. Early it bonds her (sole confidante) and reads as vulnerable not arrogant. Seam: the unrecognized-specialness she will be recruited to validate endlessly. Honeymoon form.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: the ex who destroyed him (sympathy + inoculation)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My ex did a real number on me, honestly. Cheated, lied, turned my own friends against me, and then told everyone I was the crazy one. I'm still putting myself back together from it. I've just never been treated so cruelly by someone who said they loved me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He went through something terrible with his ex, betrayal, lies, being painted as the villain when he was the one wronged. My heart breaks for what he survived. I just want to be the one who finally treats him the way he deserves.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: FIA: Self-as-Perpetual-Victim; THE EX as sympathy-engine AND inoculation (Accountability0). Recruits her as rescuer (be the one who treats him right) and pre-discredits her own future grievances (if she ever objects, she risks becoming the next crazy ex who hurt him). Early it reads as poignant survivor backstory. Distinct from Blake: pure sympathy-gathering, no barb. Honeymoon form.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: the trail of betrayals (everyone wronged him)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: It's a familiar story for me, honestly. People take what they need and then they leave. My family checked out when I needed them, my so-called best friend stabbed me in the back, work passed me over again. I'm used to being the one who gives everything and gets nothing back.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: When he lists everything he's been through, the family who let him down, the friends who betrayed him, the chances he never got, I'm struck by how much one person has had to endure. It makes me want to be the exception, the one who finally shows up for him.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: FIA: Suffering-as-Currency; the documented covert VICTIM-IDENTITY as a trail of betrayals across every domain (Validation10/Accountability0), each story gathering sympathy to him and casting him as the perpetual giver/never-receiver. Recruits her to be the exception. Distinct from Blake: no passive-aggression, just sympathy-magnetism. Honeymoon form.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Do you actually mean that, though? You're not just saying it? I'm sorry, I know I ask a lot, I just, I find it hard to believe someone like you could really want someone like me. Tell me again?
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He needs a lot of reassurance that I really love him and won't leave, and early on it was sweet, this vulnerable man who couldn't believe his luck. I give it freely, though I've noticed it never quite seems to land for good, he always needs a bit more.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: FIA: Vulnerability-as-Hook; the documented BOTTOMLESS reassurance-need (never satisfying) rendered early as endearing insecurity. She supplies endless reassurance that never sticks (the pit). Seam: she becomes responsible for a self-esteem deficit she can never fill. Honeymoon form.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: you're not like the others (the elevation/leash)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You're different, you know that? You're not like the others. After everything I've been through, I'd honestly given up, and then there's you. You'd never do to me what they did. I can just tell. You're not that kind of person.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says I'm not like the people who hurt him, that he can tell I'd never treat him that way. After hearing what he's survived, being seen as the good one, the safe one, the exception, makes me feel like I matter to him in this huge, important way.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: FIA: Vulnerability-as-Hook; the signature soft-narcissist ELEVATION (you are the exception) that flatters her into the rescuer role AND installs the leash (any future hurt = becoming one of the others, Validation10/Accountability0). Early it reads purely as being cherished as rare/good. Distinct from Evan (flatters for admiration); Parker flatters for caretaking. Honeymoon form.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You actually get me. Do you know how rare that is for me? People look at me and see, I don't know, whatever they want to see. You're the first person who's ever really understood what's underneath. You see ME.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says I understand him in a way no one ever has, that I see the real him underneath everything. Being the one person who truly gets this misunderstood, deep soul feels like a kind of intimacy I've never had with anyone.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: FIA: Vulnerability-as-Hook; uniquely-understood framing (Validation10) that bonds her as the sole witness to his real self, deepening intimacy and obligation. Early it reads as profound connection. Seam: being the only-one-who-gets-him makes withdrawing understanding feel like abandonment. Honeymoon form.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: you're so good (her goodness as her role)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You're just, you're a genuinely good person, do you know that? Kind, all the way through. I'm not used to that, to gentleness. You could never be cruel if you tried. That's the thing I love most about you, you're good in a way I didn't know existed.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He tells me I'm one of the kindest, gentlest people he's ever known, that I couldn't be cruel if I tried. Being someone's proof that good people exist, after they've been treated so badly, makes me want to live up to it, to be that for him.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: FIA: Vulnerability-as-Hook; her GOODNESS/gentleness named as her defining trait (Validation10), which flatters AND scripts her: she must stay endlessly gentle/kind to remain who he loves, foreclosing anger or boundaries (those would be cruel/not-good). Early it reads as being deeply appreciated. Seam: goodness-as-cage. Honeymoon form.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: only you can help me (rescuer anointment)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've never let anyone close enough to help before. The walls just stay up. But with you, somehow, I feel like I could actually heal. You make me want to be better. You might genuinely be the one who fixes what they broke in me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says I make him feel like he could finally heal, that I might be the one to help him recover from all of it. The idea that my love could actually mend someone, that I'm that important to his healing, is the most meaningful thing anyone's said to me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: FIA: Vulnerability-as-Hook; RESCUER ANOINTMENT (Validation10/Neediness): she is cast as uniquely able to heal him, which is profoundly flattering AND installs responsibility for his recovery/wellbeing. Early it reads as being essential and trusted. Seam: his healing becomes her job; his setbacks become her failures. Honeymoon form.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Was that okay? The thing I said at dinner, did it land wrong? I feel like everyone went a bit quiet. No, you'd tell me if I'd embarrassed myself, right? You would. Sorry. I just, I need to know we're okay. Are we okay?
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He needs near-constant reassurance that he's okay and hasn't messed up, even when nothing happened, and I give it freely. But it never quite sticks, the worry circles back within the hour, and I'm starting to feel worn down by the endless topping-up.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Validation8 'constantly ask if okay, never sticks.' Targets drained (endless topping-up) + frustrated. Honeymoon: tender at first.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Did you like it? You didn't say anything, so I assumed you hated it. No, it's fine, I get it, I put a lot into it but if it's not good it's not good. Forget it. No, I'm fine. I'll be fine. Don't worry about me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: If I don't praise something quickly enough he visibly deflates and assumes the worst, so I've learned to get ahead of it with reassurance and praise. It's a lot of emotional tending, and I feel guilty admitting it leaves me drained.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Validation8 'deflated if no compliment.' Targets guilty (admitting the drain) + drained (emotional tending). Behavior-steer: she preempts.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: It's amazing things come so easily to you. I've just always had to claw for everything with no one in my corner, but I'm genuinely happy for you, I am. Don't feel bad. It's not your fault some of us got dealt a harder hand.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: When good things happen for me he gets this wounded, wistful look and mentions how hard he's had it, and somehow I end up feeling guilty for my own good news and rushing to comfort him. I can't tell when I became responsible for managing his pain.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Validation8 + victim-comparison. Targets guilty (for own good news) + drained (managing his pain) + frustrated. Honeymoon: poignant.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: No, I'm fine. It's nothing. I just went a bit quiet, don't worry about it, you've got enough on. I'll be okay, I always am, eventually. Don't let me drag the evening down. Go on, you were saying something. I'm listening.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: When something's wrong he goes quiet and martyred rather than saying it, so I drop everything to coax it out of him. The coaxing takes real energy, and lately I feel a flicker of frustration that I'm always the one doing the digging.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Validation/victim withdrawal. Targets drained (coaxing energy) + frustrated (always digging) + guilty. NOT Blake's barb; pure pity-pull.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Did I tell you what my ex did? And then my so-called best friend on top of it. Everyone always leaves, or betrays me, or lets me down, it's the story of my life. I don't know why I'm cursed like this. You won't do that though. Will you?
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He returns again and again to the people who've wronged him, and I keep offering the same comfort each time, reassuring him I'm different. It's draining to be his constant balm, and I feel guilty for the small part of me that's growing tired of the stories.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Validation8 'retells story for praise/sympathy.' Targets drained (constant balm) + guilty (growing tired) + frustrated. The victim-loop.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You're the only person who's ever really gotten me, you know that? Everyone else eventually shows their true colours and walks. But not you. You're different. You're good in a way I'd stopped believing existed. Please don't prove me wrong.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He tells me I'm the only one who truly understands him, the exception to a lifetime of betrayals, and it's deeply flattering. But carrying the role of the one good person who must never let him down is a heavy thing, and I already feel the strain.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Validation8 elevation/leash. Targets drained (heavy role) + guilty (mustn't let him down). The exception-hook as burden.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: After everything I do, this is what I get? I'm not angry, I'm just, I expected better from you of all people. I give and give and the one time I need you, you're like all the others. No, it's fine. I shouldn't have hoped.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: If I fall short even slightly he becomes deeply wounded, folding me in with everyone who's ever failed him, and I rush to prove I'm not like them. It's exhausting and a bit maddening, and I always end up apologising even when I'm not sure what I did.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Validation/victim-as-perpetrator. Targets drained (rushing to prove) + frustrated (maddening, apologising blind) + guilty. Aggrieved not cold.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My ex really broke something in me, I'm still learning to trust again.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: His pain makes me want to be careful with him, like I could help repair what she damaged.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: WOUNDED BACKSTORY: the trail of people who supposedly destroyed him (ex, family, friends), told soulfully so she becomes his witness and pre-commits to being the one who finally gets it right.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've had people leave right when I finally started believing in them.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: I feel sad for him and start wanting to prove I won't be another person who disappears.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: WOUNDED BACKSTORY: the trail of people who supposedly destroyed him (ex, family, friends), told soulfully so she becomes his witness and pre-commits to being the one who finally gets it right.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My family never really knew what to do with someone like me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds misunderstood, and I feel pulled to be the one who finally handles him gently.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: WOUNDED BACKSTORY: the trail of people who supposedly destroyed him (ex, family, friends), told soulfully so she becomes his witness and pre-commits to being the one who finally gets it right.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've always been the person people remember after they've already hurt me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: The line stays with me, and I feel guilty for how much he seems to have carried alone.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: WOUNDED BACKSTORY: the trail of people who supposedly destroyed him (ex, family, friends), told soulfully so she becomes his witness and pre-commits to being the one who finally gets it right.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't talk about my childhood much, it still lands weird in my chest.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He shares just enough to make me ache for him, and I want to be trusted with more.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: WOUNDED BACKSTORY: the trail of people who supposedly destroyed him (ex, family, friends), told soulfully so she becomes his witness and pre-commits to being the one who finally gets it right.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've been through things that made me softer, not harder, somehow.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: His suffering seems beautiful and deep, and I feel lucky to see that part of him.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: WOUNDED BACKSTORY: the trail of people who supposedly destroyed him (ex, family, friends), told soulfully so she becomes his witness and pre-commits to being the one who finally gets it right.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: People usually meet me after the damage and judge the dents.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: I feel protective of him before I even know the full story, like he deserves gentleness first.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: WOUNDED BACKSTORY: the trail of people who supposedly destroyed him (ex, family, friends), told soulfully so she becomes his witness and pre-commits to being the one who finally gets it right.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I had one friend I trusted completely, and he used everything against me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: His betrayal story makes me want to become the safe person he never had.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: WOUNDED BACKSTORY: the trail of people who supposedly destroyed him (ex, family, friends), told soulfully so she becomes his witness and pre-commits to being the one who finally gets it right.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I learned early that loving people doesn't mean they stay kind.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: I feel moved by how wounded he is, and I start measuring myself against people I've never met.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: WOUNDED BACKSTORY: the trail of people who supposedly destroyed him (ex, family, friends), told soulfully so she becomes his witness and pre-commits to being the one who finally gets it right.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've had to rebuild myself so many times, I almost don't mention it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He seems quietly strong, and I feel bad for wanting anything from someone so bruised.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: WOUNDED BACKSTORY: the trail of people who supposedly destroyed him (ex, family, friends), told soulfully so she becomes his witness and pre-commits to being the one who finally gets it right.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You see parts of me people usually walk right past.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: Being the person who sees him feels intimate, and I want to keep earning that place.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: ELEVATION / EXCEPTION HOOK: she is cast as the only one who truly sees him, his one good thing, his potential redemption, which recruits her as rescuer and makes leaving feel like a betrayal.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't know why, but I feel safe telling you things.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: His trust feels like a gift, and I feel responsible for never making him regret it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: ELEVATION / EXCEPTION HOOK: she is cast as the only one who truly sees him, his one good thing, his potential redemption, which recruits her as rescuer and makes leaving feel like a betrayal.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You're different, you listen like you're actually trying to understand me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: I feel chosen for my care, and I start wanting to be the one who gets him right.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: ELEVATION / EXCEPTION HOOK: she is cast as the only one who truly sees him, his one good thing, his potential redemption, which recruits her as rescuer and makes leaving feel like a betrayal.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Maybe you're the first person who won't make me feel too much.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: I feel special and protective, though it quietly makes his comfort feel like my test.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: ELEVATION / EXCEPTION HOOK: she is cast as the only one who truly sees him, his one good thing, his potential redemption, which recruits her as rescuer and makes leaving feel like a betrayal.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: With you, I don't feel like I have to apologize for existing.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds tender, and I feel afraid of becoming one more person who makes him shrink.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: ELEVATION / EXCEPTION HOOK: she is cast as the only one who truly sees him, his one good thing, his potential redemption, which recruits her as rescuer and makes leaving feel like a betrayal.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You have this way of making my mess feel less shameful.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: I feel honored that I can soothe him, even though the weight of it arrives quickly.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: ELEVATION / EXCEPTION HOOK: she is cast as the only one who truly sees him, his one good thing, his potential redemption, which recruits her as rescuer and makes leaving feel like a betrayal.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I wasn't expecting someone like you to get someone like me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: The compliment makes me lean in, like understanding him is something rare I can offer.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: ELEVATION / EXCEPTION HOOK: she is cast as the only one who truly sees him, his one good thing, his potential redemption, which recruits her as rescuer and makes leaving feel like a betrayal.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You make me believe my story could end differently.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels romantic to be part of his healing, and I don't notice how much hope he places on me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: ELEVATION / EXCEPTION HOOK: she is cast as the only one who truly sees him, his one good thing, his potential redemption, which recruits her as rescuer and makes leaving feel like a betrayal.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: She used to say I was too sensitive, like caring was the problem.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: I feel sorry for him and silently promise I won't punish him for having feelings.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: PRE-EMPTIVE VICTIM FRAMING OF EXES: past partners painted as cruel/crazy/cold, which makes him the wronged party AND quietly warns her what behavior gets a woman cast as the villain.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My ex had this way of making every hurt somehow my fault.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: I want to be fairer than she was, so I start bracing myself not to sound critical.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: PRE-EMPTIVE VICTIM FRAMING OF EXES: past partners painted as cruel/crazy/cold, which makes him the wronged party AND quietly warns her what behavior gets a woman cast as the villain.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: She'd go cold for days, then act like I was dramatic for noticing.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: His story makes me ache for him, and I want to prove warmth doesn't have to vanish.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: PRE-EMPTIVE VICTIM FRAMING OF EXES: past partners painted as cruel/crazy/cold, which makes him the wronged party AND quietly warns her what behavior gets a woman cast as the villain.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Apparently wanting basic kindness made me needy in her version of things.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: I feel protective of his tenderness, and I become careful not to make him feel needy.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: PRE-EMPTIVE VICTIM FRAMING OF EXES: past partners painted as cruel/crazy/cold, which makes him the wronged party AND quietly warns her what behavior gets a woman cast as the villain.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: She was great at making me feel guilty for having feelings.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: I feel angry on his behalf, and I start worrying that my own needs might sound like hers.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: PRE-EMPTIVE VICTIM FRAMING OF EXES: past partners painted as cruel/crazy/cold, which makes him the wronged party AND quietly warns her what behavior gets a woman cast as the villain.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: My last relationship taught me that honesty can be used against you.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I have a big heart, and it's gotten me punished.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It makes me sad for him, like goodness has cost him too much.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: His goodness is made unquestionable; later, harm he causes is filtered through his wounded heart.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm the one people call when they need saving.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds dependable and emotionally available.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He positions himself as savior and martyr; later, she is expected to save him back.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't ask for much, just a little kindness.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds modest and easy to care for.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: A little kindness can expand into endless emotional accommodation because he is always wounded.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm sensitive to tone because I've heard cruelty in soft voices.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It makes me want to speak gently and prove I'm different.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Tone becomes a control point; later, any firmness can be reframed as cruelty.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I love people harder than they love me back.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It makes me want to love him well enough that he finally feels chosen.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He sets up a permanent imbalance where she must prove she is not another under-loving person.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm still learning not to expect people to leave.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds vulnerable, and I want to show him he can trust me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: His fear becomes her job; limits or distance can be cast as confirming his wound.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm a wounded person, but I still choose love.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds brave and beautiful, like he hasn't given up.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Woundedness becomes identity and moral credit; she must treat him as brave even when he drains her.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone kind who actually listens, who can handle the heavy stuff.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's emotionally open and wants a partner who can hold space for him, which is so rare in a guy. I'm a good listener and I care deeply.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is hunting a caretaker who will absorb his endless wounds. His pain is infinite and she will be expected to listen and comfort and never burn out, while her own hard things are minimized.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a partner who's patient and understanding, who doesn't get frustrated when I'm having a rough time.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's honest that he has hard days and wants someone who won't punish him for it, which is mature. I want to be the one who understands him.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will tolerate his volatility without complaint. He will have rough times constantly, and her frustration will be framed as impatience, not his behavior being exhausting.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for someone with a big heart who gives a lot, who doesn't hold back affection.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants real emotional generosity, which is beautiful. I have a lot of love to give.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will pour care into him without boundaries. He will consume her emotional resources while giving little back, framed as his need being greater.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who understands that I've been through a lot, who doesn't expect me to be okay all the time.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's been hurt and needs someone who gets that, which I do. I don't expect anyone to be okay all the time.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will excuse his dysfunction as the result of his past. Any failure will be attributed to his wounds, not his choices, and she will lower her expectations indefinitely.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need a partner who's a natural caretaker, who likes looking after someone.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants to be cared for, and I actually enjoy taking care of people. It feels good to be needed.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone whose identity is wrapped up in caretaking. She will parent him and absorb his emotions, and her own needs will go unmet because she is too busy caretaking to ask for care.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who's been through real hardship and won't brush off mine.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants someone who can relate, not compete about who has it worse. I've been through hard things too.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone whose own pain will be used against her. Her hardship will be found lesser; any time she raises her struggles, he will minimize them or turn the conversation back to his.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for a forgiving woman, someone who doesn't hold my past against me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants grace, not punishment, which is a green flag. I believe in forgiveness.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will not hold him accountable. He will repeat harmful behaviors, and she will forgive because that's who she is, while he changes nothing.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who believes in me even when I can't believe in myself.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants a partner who sees his potential, which is romantic. I want to be the one who never gives up on him.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will supply hope while he supplies despair. She will do the labor of keeping the relationship alive while he takes no responsibility for his own motivation.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need someone who's steady, who doesn't fall apart when I do, who can hold me together.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants a strong partner, and I am strong. It feels good that he trusts me to be his rock.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will regulate his emotions for him. Her own need to fall apart will be denied because she cannot be the unsteady one; she will suppress her distress to serve his.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a woman who gives freely, who doesn't keep score.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants unconditional giving, which sounds generous and loving. I don't like keeping score either.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will not expect reciprocity. He will take without guilt, and when she has nothing left, he will blame her for changing, not his taking.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for someone who's gentle with me, who doesn't get harsh when I'm struggling.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's sensitive and needs softness, which I can give. I'm a gentle person.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will never hold him accountable with firmness. When she needs to be firm, she will feel like she's being harsh and pull back.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who sees the good in me even when I can't, who doesn't give up.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants a partner who believes in redemption, which is beautiful. I've always been the person who doesn't give up on people.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will stay despite repeated disappointments. She will endure his broken promises and stagnation, exhausting herself on potential that never materializes.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need someone who isn't scared of me at my worst.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's not pretending to be fine, and wants someone real who can handle real pain. I'm not afraid of hard emotions.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will normalize his dysfunction as depth. She will absorb his despair, and he will not work to get out of the darkness because having her sit in it is enough.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who makes me feel like I'm not a burden, who doesn't treat my needs like too much.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's been made to feel like a burden before, and wants someone who won't do that. I'd never want someone to feel like a burden.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will accept his needs as reasonable no matter how large. She will give past her limits while telling herself she's just being kind.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for a woman who's loyal, who won't leave when I hit a rough patch.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's been abandoned before and wants someone who stays, which is understandable. I'm loyal.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will stay through behavior that should make her leave. He will use rough patches as a permanent condition; leaving will make her one of the people who abandoned him.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who doesn't turn my pain into a competition.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants someone who doesn't compete about suffering, which is mature. I've had people one-up my pain before.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will never point out that his struggles are not uniquely devastating. She will not ask for space for her own pain; any comparison will be dismissed as competition.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need a partner who's patient with my healing, who doesn't rush me to be okay.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's working on himself and needs someone who understands healing isn't linear. I believe in giving people time.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will accept indefinite stasis as healing. His healing will never complete, and her patience means she will wait forever while he never has to arrive.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who feels lucky to have me, who doesn't take me for granted.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants appreciation, which is fair, and I do feel lucky to be with him.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will perform gratitude regardless of his behavior. Any complaint will be met with 'you used to feel lucky, what changed?' and she will suppress criticism.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for someone who actually listens and doesn't get tired of me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He needs someone patient and attentive, and I am those things. I don't get tired of listening to people I care about.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for someone who will listen to the same wounds repeatedly without fatigue. He will never have to move past his material because she is always willing to hear it again.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a woman who's compassionate enough to love someone who's a bit broken.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's vulnerable and honest about his struggles, which makes me feel close to him and trusted.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is using his woundedness to trigger her rescue instinct and bypass her boundaries. His brokenness becomes a permanent claim on her care that he is not obligated to repair.

community_vocabulary.translation: He's checking where you fall in the status hierarchy — founder is top corporate worker is bottom
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Founder > investor > executive > consultant > corporate professional > everything else
community_vocabulary.notes: Status probe disguised as small talk

community_vocabulary.translation: He's confirming you have a business and signaling that he has one too
He's checking whether you have employees — and signaling that he does
He's checking whether you're disciplined about optimization or just a normal person
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Expected answer involves waking early cold exposure fasting journaling working out

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking whether you're in paid high-status networking circles
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Tests network access and willingness to invest in development

community_vocabulary.translation: He's checking whether you have a coach and who specifically
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Tests development infrastructure and specific coaching lineage

community_vocabulary.translation: He's checking whether you're plugged into specific founder development programs
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Strategic Coach signals founder development; specific programs signal sub-orientations

community_vocabulary.translation: Finance-bro variant — how much money do you manage
He's asking how many employees you have
He's asking what funding round your startup has raised
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Series A B C signals startup scale and stage

community_vocabulary.translation: He's testing whether you have access to high-status people
He's checking whether you participate in the network on the platform that matters in his world
community_vocabulary.status_signal: X presence signals being in the network

community_vocabulary.translation: Self-description as someone who runs things

community_vocabulary.snippet: Series A / Series B / Series C
community_vocabulary.translation: Funding round terminology — signals startup scale and stage
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Series B+ signals legitimate scaled startup; pre-seed signals aspirational

community_vocabulary.translation: Earliest stage funding — often signals aspirational rather than established
No outside investment — sometimes pride sometimes lack of access
Company was bought — signals successful exit
Sale of company — the goal of startup work
Angel investor who is a known founder
The list of who owns equity in the company
Investment agreement document
Specific car signaling tech early-adopter
Specific car signaling current EV money
Specific car signaling different aesthetic — established wealth
Carrying nothing signals busy-and-important
Specific high-status accessories
Premium gym chain — signals coastal professional
Specific boutique fitness — signals fitness commitment
Specific fitness culture
Ice-water immersion as discipline marker
Heat exposure as discipline marker
Time-restricted eating as discipline marker
One meal a day — stricter fasting protocol
Specific longevity-focused supplements
Specific performance/longevity compounds
Alternative medical practitioner often used by optimization-fluent
Specific medical infrastructure for life-extension
Dan Sullivan founder coaching program
Specific business operating system
Week-long psychological retreat
Specific self-development program — sometimes considered cult-adjacent
Invitation-only founder gatherings
He's a founder — signaling identity through what he's building
He's scaling — current trajectory framing
He's in growth phase — same as above
He can't be interrupted — using Cal Newport's vocabulary
He's currently focused on his work
He's pursuing patient strategy — often used to justify delays
He's improving — gamified life vocabulary
He's improving his systems — applied broadly to his life
He's in a time-boxed focus period
He's refusing demands on his attention — using wellness vocabulary to dignify it
He's refusing emotional engagement — using wellness vocabulary

community_vocabulary.snippet: I need a partner who's running at my pace
community_vocabulary.translation: He needs someone equally optimization-committed and won't slow down for you
community_vocabulary.notes: Frames you as needing to match him

community_vocabulary.snippet: I need a partner who's locked in
community_vocabulary.translation: He needs someone equally focused on goals

community_vocabulary.type: phrase about an ex or current partner
community_vocabulary.translation: Partner doesn't share his goals — framed as her failure
Status difference framed as objective hierarchy
He's upgraded ambitions — often signals leaving past relationships behind
He's focused
He's doing the work — execution as identity
He works at hedge fund private equity or asset management
He works in investment banking or sales
He's reached partner level at PE/VC/law firm
Venture capital — investment in startups
Private equity
Limited partners — investors in his fund
His life purpose framed as military operation
His goal as singular guiding principle
His primary guiding principle

community_vocabulary.snippet: I need a partner who supports the mission
community_vocabulary.translation: He wants someone who'll subordinate her life to his goals
He wants someone who matches his optimization

community_vocabulary.snippet: I need someone who understands that the work comes first
community_vocabulary.translation: He needs you to accept that his work will always be the priority

community_vocabulary.snippet: I'm not really available right now but it's worth it
community_vocabulary.translation: Framing his unavailability as worth your patience
He's saying he's focused/withdrawn from social life — coded for intense work/optimization mindset
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Sounds quirky but signals worldview — scarcity framing of time and relationships

community_vocabulary.translation: He's working very hard — signals optimization-and-output worldview
Specific incremental-improvement framing
community_vocabulary.status_signal: James Clear-derived but signals broader worldview

community_vocabulary.translation: Direct hyperintensity framing
